info.title: Amazon EC2 Container Service Tools for .NET Core applications (3.5.2)
info.description:
Project Home: https://github.com/aws/aws-extensions-for-dotnet-cli
Commands to deploy to Amazon EC2 Container Service:
deploy-service          Push the application to ECR and runs the application as a long lived service on the ECS Cluster.
deploy-task             Push the application to ECR and then runs it as a task on the ECS Cluster.
deploy-scheduled-task   Push the application to ECR and then sets up CloudWatch Event Schedule rule to run the application.
Commands to manage docker images to Amazon EC2 Container Registry:
push-image              Build Docker image and push the image to Amazon ECR.
To get help on individual commands execute:
dotnet ecs help <command>

commands.name: deploy-service
commands.description: Push the application to ECR and runs the application as a long lived service on the ECS Cluster.

options.name: --disable-interactive
options.description: When set to true missing required parameters will not be prompted for.

options.name: --region
options.description: The region to connect to AWS services, if not set region will be detected from the environment.

options.name: --profile
options.description: Profile to use to look up AWS credentials, if not set environment credentials will be used.

options.name: --profile-location
options.description: Optional override to the search location for Profiles, points at a shared credentials file.

options.name: --aws-access-key-id
options.description: The AWS access key id. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --aws-secret-key
options.description: The AWS secret key. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --aws-session-token
options.description: The AWS session token. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: -pl
options.description: | --project-location             The location of the project, if not set the current directory will be assumed.

options.name: -cfg
options.description: | --config-file                  Configuration file storing default values for command line arguments.

options.name: -pcfg
options.description: | --persist-config-file          If true the arguments used for a successful deployment are persisted to a config file.

options.name: -c
options.description: | --configuration                Configuration to build with, for example Release or Debug.

options.name: -f
options.description: | --framework                    Target framework to compile, for example netcoreapp3.1.

options.name: -t
options.description: | --tag                          Name and optionally a tag in the 'name:tag' format.

options.name: -dbwd
options.description: | --docker-build-working-dir     The directory to execute the "docker build" command from.

options.name: -dbo
options.description: | --docker-build-options         Additional options passed to the "docker build" command.

options.name: -sip
options.description: | --skip-image-push              Skip building and push an image to Amazon ECR.

options.name: -lt
options.description: | --launch-type                  The launch type on which to run tasks. Valid values EC2 | FARGATE.

options.name: -ls
options.description: | --launch-subnets               Comma delimited list of subnet ids used when launch type is FARGATE

options.name: -lsg
options.description: | --launch-security-groups       Comma delimited list of security group ids used when launch type is FARGATE

options.name: --assign-public-ip
options.description: If true a public IP address is assigned to the task when launch type is FARGATE

options.name: -ec
options.description: | --cluster                      Name of the ECS Cluster to run the docker image.

options.name: -cs
options.description: | --cluster-service              Name of the service to run on the ECS Cluster.

options.name: -dc
options.description: | --desired-count                The number of instantiations of the task to place and keep running in your service. Default is 1.

options.name: --deployment-maximum-percent
options.description: The upper limit of the number of tasks that are allowed in the RUNNING or PENDING state in a service during a deployment.

options.name: --deployment-minimum-healthy-percent
options.description: The lower limit of the number of running tasks that must remain in the RUNNING state in a service during a deployment.

options.name: --placement-constraints
options.description: Placement constraint to use for tasks in service. Format is <type>=<optional expression>,...

options.name: --placement-strategy
options.description: Placement strategy to use for tasks in service. Format is <type>=<optional field>,...

options.name: --elb-service-role
options.description: The name or (ARN) of the IAM role that allows ECS to make calls to the load balancer.

options.name: -etg
options.description: | --elb-target-group             The full Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Elastic Load Balancing target group associated with a service.

options.name: -ecp
options.description: | --elb-container-port           The port on the container to associate with the load balancer.

options.name: --task-definition-name
options.description: Name of the ECS Task Defintion to be created or updated.

options.name: --task-definition-network-mode
options.description: The Docker networking mode to use for the containers in the task.

options.name: --task-definition-task-role
options.description: The IAM role that will provide AWS credentials for the containers in the Task Definition.

options.name: --task-execution-role
options.description: The IAM role ECS assumes to pull images from ECR and publish logs to CloudWatch Logs. Fargate only.

options.name: --task-cpu
options.description: The amount of cpu to allocate for the task definition. Fargate only.

options.name: --task-memory
options.description: The amount of memory to allocated for the task definition. Fargate only.

options.name: --task-definition-volumes
options.description: Volume definitions that containers in your task may use. Format is JSON string.

options.name: --platform-version
options.description: The platform version selected for the task. Fargate only.

options.name: --container-command
options.description: A comma delimited list of commands to pass to the container.

options.name: --container-cpu
options.description: The number of cpu units reserved for the container.

options.name: --container-disable-networking
options.description: When this parameter is true, networking is disabled within the container.

options.name: --container-dns-search-domains
options.description: A comma delimited of DNS search domains that are presented to the container.

options.name: --container-dns-servers
options.description: A comma delimited of DNS servers that are presented to the container.

options.name: --container-docker-labels
options.description: Labels to add to the container. Format is <key1>=<value1>;<key2>=<value2>.

options.name: --container-docker-security-options
options.description: A list of strings to provide custom labels for SELinux and AppArmor multi-level security systems.

options.name: --container-entry-point
options.description: The entry point that is passed to the container.

options.name: --container-environment-variables
options.description: Environment variables for a container definition. Format is <key1>=<value1>;<key2>=<value2>.

options.name: --container-is-essential
options.description: If true, and that container fails, all other containers that are part of the task are stopped.

options.name: --container-extra-hosts
options.description: Hostnames and IP address entries that are added to the /etc/hosts file of a container. Format is JSON string.

options.name: --container-hostname
options.description: The hostname to use for your container.

options.name: --container-links
options.description: Comma delimited list of container names to communicate without the need for port mapping.

options.name: --container-linux-parameters
options.description: The Linux capabilities for the container that are added to or dropped. Format is JSON string.

options.name: --container-log-configuration
options.description: The log driver to use for the container. Format is JSON string.

options.name: --container-memory-hard-limit
options.description: The hard limit (in MiB) of memory to present to the container.

options.name: --container-memory-soft-limit
options.description: The soft limit (in MiB) of memory to reserve for the container.

options.name: --container-mount-points
options.description: The mount points for data volumes in your container. Format is JSON string.

options.name: --container-name
options.description: Name of the Container in a Task Definition to be created/updated.

options.name: --container-port-mapping
options.description: The mapping of ports. Format is <host-port>:<container-port>,...

options.name: --container-privileged
options.description: If true, the container is given elevated privileges on the host container instance

options.name: --container-readonly-root-filesystem
options.description: If true, the container is given read-only access to its root file system.

options.name: --container-ulimits
options.description: The ulimit settings to pass to the container. Format is JSON string.

options.name: --container-user
options.description: The user name to use inside the container.

options.name: --container-working-directory
options.description: The working directory in which to run commands inside the container.

options.description: The name of the target that will be assigned to the rule and point to the ECS task definition.

options.name: --schedule-expression
options.description: The scheduling expression. For example, "cron(0 20 * * ? *)" or "rate(5 minutes)".

options.name: --cloudwatch-event-role
options.description: The role that IAM will assume to invoke the target.

commands.name: push-image
commands.description:
Build Docker image and push the image to Amazon ECR.
To get help on individual commands execute:
dotnet ecs help <command>

options.name: --disable-interactive
options.description: When set to true missing required parameters will not be prompted for.

options.name: --region
options.description: The region to connect to AWS services, if not set region will be detected from the environment.

options.name: --profile
options.description: Profile to use to look up AWS credentials, if not set environment credentials will be used.

options.name: --profile-location
options.description: Optional override to the search location for Profiles, points at a shared credentials file.

options.name: --aws-access-key-id
options.description: The AWS access key id. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --aws-secret-key
options.description: The AWS secret key. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: --aws-session-token
options.description: The AWS session token. Used when setting credentials explicitly instead of using --profile.

options.name: -pl
options.description: | --project-location             The location of the project, if not set the current directory will be assumed.

options.name: -cfg
options.description: | --config-file                  Configuration file storing default values for command line arguments.

options.name: -pcfg
options.description: | --persist-config-file          If true the arguments used for a successful deployment are persisted to a config file.

options.name: -c
options.description: | --configuration                Configuration to build with, for example Release or Debug.

options.name: -f
options.description: | --framework                    Target framework to compile, for example netcoreapp3.1.

options.name: -po
options.description: | --publish-options              Additional options passed to the "dotnet publish" command.

options.name: --docker-host-build-output-dir
options.description: If set a "dotnet publish" command is executed on the host machine before executing "docker build". The output can be copied into image being built.

options.name: -ldi
options.description: | --local-docker-image           If set the docker build command is skipped and the indicated local image is pushed to ECR.

options.name: -df
options.description: | --dockerfile                   The docker file used to build the image. Default value is "Dockerfile".

options.name: -it
options.description: | --image-tag                    Name and optionally a tag in the 'name:tag' format.

options.name: -t
options.description: | --tag                          Obsolete. This has been replaced with the --image-tag switch.

options.name: -dbwd
options.description: | --docker-build-working-dir     The directory to execute the "docker build" command from.

options.name: -dbo
options.description: | --docker-build-options         Additional options passed to the "docker build" command.
